acceptance_criteria: Magic byte validation rejects mislabeled files; video rejected; over-limit rejected; 202 shape matches mastodon.social oracle; client filename not used in storage path
description:
Accept multipart/form-data with 'file' field and optional 'description' and 'focus' fields. Validate: MIME type must be image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp, or image/heic (check magic bytes, NOT just file extension or Content-Type header — never trust client-declared MIME). File size must not exceed max_media_mb from config. Reject video with 422. Store original to disk at media/{id[0..2]}/{id}.{ext}. Insert media row with post_id=NULL. Spawn async processing task. Return 202 with partial MediaAttachment.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to collect POST /api/v2/media responses from mastodon.social and document the 202 partial response shape. Spawn a parallel subagent to research magic byte detection for image types (do NOT trust Content-Type header — clients and proxies may mislabel).

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Upload valid JPEG → 202 with partial attachment, processing starts
- Upload valid PNG → 202
- Upload file with .jpg extension but MP4 magic bytes → 422 (magic byte check, not extension)
- Upload file labeled image/jpeg but actually a shell script → 422
- Upload over size limit → 422 with clear error
- Upload video/mp4 → 422 with 'video not supported' error
- Verify 202 response shape matches mastodon.social oracle (id, url=null, preview_url=null, blurhash=null)

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- Magic byte detection: first 8 bytes sufficient for JPEG (FFD8FF), PNG (89504E47), GIF (47494638), WebP (52494646...57454250), HEIC (check ftyp box)
- Storage path: sanitize derived filename, use only the ID (snowflake) — never use client-provided filename
- Prevent directory traversal: storage path must be computed as media_dir + id[0..2] + '/' + id + '.' + ext — no user input in path
- Rate limit: max 10 uploads per minute per account
title: POST /api/v2/media: multipart upload, MIME validation, 202 Accepted
